converter.title: Kitchen Unit Converter
converter.description: Convert grams, ounces, liters, tablespoons and other kitchen measurements instantly.

forWhom.title: Who is this for
forWhom.text: Especially useful when working with international recipes that use different measurement systems: metric (grams, liters) and imperial (ounces, pounds).
items: Professional cooks
Pastry chefs
Food bloggers
Home cooks
Culinary students

seoText.title: Kitchen Unit Converter
seoText.p1: Precision in measurement is a cornerstone of professional cooking. Different countries use different systems — metric and imperial — which can cause confusion when working with international recipes.
seoText.p2: European recipes typically use grams and milliliters, while American recipes rely on ounces, pounds, cups, and tablespoons. This converter bridges both worlds instantly.
seoText.p3: Convert: grams to ounces, kilograms to pounds, milliliters to liters, liters to fluid ounces, cups to milliliters, tablespoons to teaspoons — all in one place.
seoText.p4: The tool is especially useful for chefs working with international menus, culinary students, food bloggers, and anyone who wants to cook precisely.

items.q: How to convert grams to ounces?
items.a: 1 ounce = 28.35 grams. Divide grams by 28.35 to get ounces.

items.q: How many grams in a pound?
How many ml in a cup?
How many teaspoons in a tablespoon?

closingText.title: Unit calculator for cooks
closingText.text: This kitchen unit converter helps you quickly convert measurements used in different countries. European recipes use grams and milliliters, while American recipes use ounces, pounds, and cups. Use this tool to convert ingredients on the fly while cooking.

fishSeason.title: Fish Season Calendar
fishSeason.description: When is fish at its best? Monthly guide to freshness and availability of fish and seafood.

ingredientAnalyzer.title: Nutrition Table & Ingredient Analyzer

ingredientsCatalog.title: Ingredient Catalog

ingredientConverter.title: Ingredient Converter
ingredientConverter.description: 1 cup of flour in grams? 2 tbsp of butter in oz? Pick an ingredient and units — the converter uses real density.
ingredientConverter.microtrust: Results calculated based on real ingredient density

popularConversions.title: Popular Kitchen Conversions
popularConversions.description: 1 cup of flour in grams, 1 tablespoon of butter in grams and other popular cooking conversions.
popularConversions.noData: No data available. Try again later.
popularConversions.seoP1: How many grams in a cup of flour? How much does a tablespoon of butter weigh? We answer these questions with pre-calculated conversions for popular kitchen ingredients.
popularConversions.seoP2: The table covers flour, sugar, butter, oil, honey and more. All values account for ingredient density.
popularConversions.seoP3: This page is automatically updated from our API — always current data.
popularConversions.closingText: Popular kitchen conversions is a collection of the most frequently searched cooking unit conversions. Ideal for chefs, pastry chefs, and food bloggers.

scale.title: Ingredient Scaler
scale.description: Scale ingredient amounts to a different number of portions — automatically.
scale.errorGeneral: Could not calculate. Please try again.
scale.seoP1: Have a recipe for 4 servings but cooking for 10? The ingredient scaler automatically recalculates amounts.
scale.seoP2: Enter the ingredient, amount, unit and portion count — the calculator adjusts the proportion and suggests an optimal unit.
scale.closingText: The ingredient scaler helps recalculate recipes for any number of servings. Perfect when cooking for a crowd or scaling down a recipe.

nutrition.title: Nutrition Calculator
nutrition.description: Calories, protein, fat and carbs for any ingredient — with localized names and photos.
nutrition.errorGeneral: Could not calculate. Please try again.
nutrition.seoP1: How many calories in 100g of flour? What is the macro breakdown of salmon? The nutrition calculator answers these questions — enter an ingredient and unit.
nutrition.seoP2: Data comes from our catalog database with real product photos and localized names in 4 languages.
nutrition.closingText: The nutrition calculator helps you quickly check calories, protein, fat and carbs for any cooking ingredient. An ideal tool for chefs and nutritionists.

suggestions.title: How many grams in a cup?
suggestions.description: Gram table — how much does a cup, tablespoon or teaspoon of various food products weigh.
suggestions.noData: No data available. Try again later.
suggestions.seoP1: How many grams in a cup of flour? How much does a tablespoon of sugar weigh? The table contains conversions for the most popular products.
suggestions.seoP2: All values account for ingredient density — data from our catalog with real photos.
suggestions.closingText: The gram table for kitchen ingredients helps quickly check ingredient weights in cups and spoons. Perfect when cooking from American recipes.

equivalents.title: Ingredient Equivalents
equivalents.description: Convert any ingredient to all measurement units — accounting for product density.
equivalents.errorGeneral: Could not convert. Please try again.
equivalents.seoP1: How many grams in a cup of flour? And how many ounces? The converter shows ingredient values in all units at once.
equivalents.seoP2: The tool uses product density from our catalog — results are more accurate than pure volume conversions.
equivalents.closingText: The ingredient equivalents converter shows how much a product weighs in grams, ounces, pounds, milliliters and cups — accounting for real density.

recipeAnalyzer.title: Recipe Analyzer
recipeAnalyzer.description: Add ingredients to get instant nutrition analysis, flavor radar chart, and smart suggestions to improve your recipe.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP1: The Recipe Analyzer combines three professional culinary tools in one interface: a nutrition calculator, a flavor profiler, and a recipe optimizer.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP2: Enter your ingredients with amounts in grams. The tool fetches real nutritional data from our catalog of 112+ ingredients and calculates total calories, protein, fat, carbohydrates, fiber, and sugar.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP3: The Flavor Radar Chart visualizes your recipe across 6 taste dimensions: sweetness, acidity, bitterness, umami, fat, and aroma. A balanced recipe scores higher.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP4: Based on the analysis, the Suggestion Engine recommends ingredients that fill flavor gaps, have high pairing scores, and add nutritional value.
recipeAnalyzer.seoP5: This tool is free and works with real data — no guessing, no AI hallucinations.
recipeAnalyzer.changeConcept: For further improvement, try changing ingredients manually

flavorPairing.title: Flavor Pairing Explorer
flavorPairing.description: Search any ingredient to discover its best flavor matches, culinary profile, and pairing scores.
flavorPairing.noPairings: No pairing data available for this ingredient yet.
flavorPairing.seoP1: Flavor pairing is a culinary concept based on the idea that ingredients sharing similar flavor compounds taste good together. Our pairing database combines three scoring dimensions:
flavorPairing.seoFlavor: Flavor Score — how well the ingredients complement each other across taste dimensions (sweetness, acidity, umami, bitterness, fat, aroma)
flavorPairing.seoNutrition: Nutrition Score — whether the combination creates a nutritionally balanced pairing
flavorPairing.seoCulinary: Culinary Score — based on traditional culinary traditions and professional cooking experience
flavorPairing.seoP2: The Pair Score is a weighted average of all three dimensions. Scores range from 0 to 10, where 10 is a perfect pairing.
flavorPairing.seoP3: Click on any pairing result to explore that ingredient further — navigate the entire flavor graph by following connections.
flavorPairing.seoP4: Our database contains 112+ ingredients with verified pairing data. This tool is free, fast, and based on real culinary data.

lab.title: Culinary Intelligence Lab
lab.description: Combine recipe analysis, nutrition, flavor profiling, and ingredient pairing in one powerful interface.
lab.subtitle: Analyze recipes, convert kitchen units, explore ingredient flavors and discover the best pairings.
lab.inputHint: e.g. 2 cups pasta, 3 tomatoes, 1 tbsp olive oil, basil
lab.noIngredients: Add some ingredients to begin analysis. Our system will calculate everything for you.
lab.clickIngredient: Click any ingredient to see its best pairings
lab.noPairings: No pairing data for this ingredient yet.
lab.seoTitle: What is the Culinary Intelligence Lab?
lab.seoP1: The Culinary Intelligence Lab combines five professional culinary tools in one interface: unit conversion, nutrition analysis, flavor profiling, recipe optimization, and ingredient pairing.
lab.seoP2: Enter your ingredients — the tool converts units, calculates nutrition, maps your flavor profile across 6 taste dimensions, and recommends ingredients to improve balance.
lab.seoP3: Click any ingredient to explore its pairing graph. Navigate from ingredient to ingredient discovering optimal combinations.
lab.seoP4: Our database contains 112+ ingredients with verified nutritional data and pairing scores. Free, fast, and based on real culinary science.

rules.lowAcidityDesc: The recipe lacks brightness. Adding an acid source will lift the flavor.
rules.fixAddAcid: Add an acid source like lemon, vinegar or tomato
rules.lowUmamiDesc: The recipe lacks depth and savory notes.
rules.fixAddUmami: Add umami-rich ingredients like cheese, soy sauce, or mushrooms
rules.lowFatDesc: The dish may feel thin. Adding a fat source will improve mouthfeel.
rules.fixAddFat: Add butter, olive oil, or cream
rules.lowAromaDesc: The recipe could use more aromatic complexity.
rules.fixAddAroma: Add garlic, basil, or fresh herbs
rules.lowSweetnessDesc: A touch of natural sweetness could balance the flavor.
rules.fixAddSweet: Add honey, onion, or carrot for natural sweetness
rules.lowBitternessDesc: A hint of bitterness adds complexity.
rules.fixAddBitter: Try black pepper, arugula, or dark greens
rules.highCarbsDesc: Over 65% of calories come from carbohydrates.
rules.fixReduceCarbs: Add protein-rich ingredients to balance macros
rules.lowProteinDesc: Under 12% of calories from protein — consider adding a protein source.
rules.fixAddProtein: Add chicken, egg, fish, or legumes
rules.highFatRatioDesc: Over 45% of calories come from fat.
rules.fixReduceFat: Reduce oil or butter, add lean ingredients
rules.fixAddFiber: Add broccoli, lentils, or whole grains
rules.highSugarDesc: Over 20% of calories come from sugar.
rules.dominanceDesc: One ingredient makes up over 60% of total weight.
rules.fixAddVariety: Add more ingredients for better balance
rules.missingFatDesc: The recipe has no oil, butter, or dairy for richness.
rules.fixAddFatSource: Add olive oil, butter, or cream
rules.missingAromaticsDesc: No herbs or spices detected. Aromatics add complexity.
rules.fixAddAromatics: Add garlic, basil, or black pepper
rules.missingProteinDesc: A grain-based recipe without meat, fish, egg, or legume.
rules.missingAcidDesc: Acidity is low and no obvious acid source found.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Professional articles about sushi, Japanese cuisine, and culinary techniques

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Sushi Chef & Food Technologist

hero.lead: I am a sushi chef and food technologist. I help restaurants build quality, processes, and menus based on product, seasonality, and precision of technique.

journey.title: My Journey
journey.content:
I am a professional sushi chef and food technologist with over 20 years of experience in gastronomy. My journey began with classical culinary education and led to work in restaurants and production kitchens across Poland, Lithuania, Estonia, Germany, France, and Canada.

Over the years of practice, I have built a deep understanding of Japanese cuisine, product handling, and professional kitchen processes — from traditional techniques to modern technological solutions.

expertise.title: Expertise
expertise.subtitle: Key areas of specialization and techniques
items: Traditional sushi preparation and presentation
Professional Japanese knife work
Selection, processing, and quality control of fish and seafood
Development and implementation of new products
Setting up and optimizing kitchen processes
Kitchen automation and gastronomic technologies
Staff training and culinary mentoring
Working with HACCP standards

fishInHouse.title: FISH in HOUSE — Dnipro
responsibilities: Quality control and shelf life extension

mission.title: My Mission
mission.content:
Through this blog and projects, I share practical knowledge of professional kitchens. My goal is to help chefs, restaurants, and food enthusiasts understand the product, technique, and technology that modern gastronomy is built on.

I believe that a strong kitchen starts not with a recipe, but with understanding the process.

about.gallery_subtitle: Selected works & moments in the kitchen

contact.title: Contact
contact.location: Based in Gdansk, Poland. Working Globally.

footer.stats: Visited by chefs and restaurateurs from different countries

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: Learn how dima-fomin.pl collects, uses and protects your information. GDPR compliant.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how dima-fomin.pl collects, uses and protects your information when you use this website.

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.text: The website administrator and personal data controller is:
items: Dima Fomin
Website: https://dima-fomin.pl
Email: fodi85999@gmail.com
Location: Gdansk, Poland

collect.title: Information We Collect
collect.text: The website may collect limited information such as:
items: Technical information about your device and browser
Anonymous analytics data (page views, session duration)
Cookies that improve website functionality
Information you voluntarily provide via contact forms

usage.title: How We Use Information
items: Improve website performance and user experience
Analyze traffic and usage patterns
Ensure website security and prevent abuse
Respond to your inquiries

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.text:
The website may use technical cookies necessary for the proper functioning of the site, security, and saving user preferences.

For website traffic analysis, Vercel Web Analytics may be used. According to Vercel's documentation, this service does not use third-party cookies and processes anonymized website visit data.
items: Essential cookies required for website functionality
Preference cookies to remember your settings

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.text: The website may use third-party technical infrastructure and analytics services. These services may process limited technical data in accordance with their own privacy policies.
items: Vercel — hosting, content delivery, and technical infrastructure of the website
Vercel Web Analytics — anonymized website traffic analysis without third-party cookies

storage.title: Data Storage
storage.text:
We store data only for the period necessary to achieve the purposes of processing.
Data submitted through contact forms is stored for the time necessary to process the request and respond to the user, unless longer retention is required by law.
Anonymized analytics data is processed in accordance with the policies of the technical services used.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.text: We process data based on the following legal grounds under GDPR:
items: Your consent — for cookies and analytics (Article 6(1)(a) GDPR)
Legitimate interest — ensuring website security and functionality (Article 6(1)(f) GDPR)

rights.title: Your Rights (GDPR)
rights.text: If you are located in the European Economic Area, you have the following rights under GDPR:
items: Right to access — request a copy of your data
Right to rectification — request correction of inaccurate data
Right to erasure — request deletion of your data
Right to restrict processing — limit how your data is used
Right to object — object to data processing
Right to data portability — receive your data in a portable format
Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
rights.supervisory:
You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority.
In Poland, the supervisory authority is:
Urząd Ochrony Danych Osobowych (UODO)
https://uodo.gov.pl

contact.title: Contact
contact.text:
If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or want to exercise your GDPR rights, please contact the website administrator:

Dima Fomin
Email: fodi85999@gmail.com

meta.description: Terms and conditions for using dima-fomin.pl website and services.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By accessing and using this website (dima-fomin.pl), you accept and agree to be bound by the following terms and conditions.

usage.title: Use of Website
usage.text: This website provides culinary content, chef tools, and professional resources. You agree to use the website only for lawful purposes.
items: Content is provided for informational purposes only
Tools are provided as-is for educational and professional use
You must not misuse or attempt to disrupt the website

content.title: Content
content.text: All recipes, articles, and tools are created for educational and professional purposes. While we strive for accuracy, we cannot guarantee that all information is error-free.

intellectual.title: Intellectual Property
intellectual.text: All content on this website, including text, images, and design, is the property of Dima Fomin unless otherwise stated.
items: Content may not be reproduced without permission
You may share links to our content freely
Attribution is required when referencing our materials

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.text: The website content is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. Nutritional calculations, unit conversions, and other tools are approximate and should be verified independently for professional use.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.text: We are not liable for any damages arising from the use of this website or reliance on its content. Use all tools and information at your own discretion.

changes.title: Changes to Terms
changes.text: We reserve the right to update these terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date.

governing.title: Governing Law
governing.text: These terms are governed by the laws of Poland and the European Union, including GDPR regulations.

meta.title: Cookie Policy
meta.description: Information about cookies used on dima-fomin.pl and how to manage them.

cookiesPage.title: Cookie Policy
cookiesPage.intro: This Cookie Policy explains what cookies are, how we use them on dima-fomin.pl, and how you can manage your cookie preferences.

what.title: What Are Cookies
what.text: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the website remember your preferences and improve your browsing experience.

types.title: Types of Cookies We Use
types.text: We use the following types of cookies:
items: Essential cookies — required for basic website functionality
Analytics cookies — help us understand how visitors use the site
Preference cookies — remember your settings and choices

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.text: These cookies are necessary for the website to function properly. They include:
items: Cookie consent preference (cookie-consent)
Theme preference (light/dark mode)
Language preference

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.text: We use Vercel Analytics to understand website traffic. These cookies collect anonymous data such as page views and session duration. Analytics cookies are only loaded if you accept cookies through the consent banner.

manage.title: Managing Cookies
manage.text: You can manage your cookie preferences in several ways:
items: Use the cookie consent banner when first visiting the site
Clear cookies through your browser settings
Disable cookies in your browser preferences
Use browser extensions that block cookies

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.text: Some third-party services may set their own cookies. We use minimal third-party services and do not use advertising cookies or tracking pixels.

updates.title: Updates to This Policy
updates.text: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated date.

rules.lowAcidityDesc: The dish lacks sourness — try adding lemon, vinegar, or tomatoes.
rules.lowUmamiDesc: Not enough savory depth — consider soy sauce, parmesan, or mushrooms.
rules.lowFatDesc: The dish needs more richness — add olive oil, butter, or avocado.
rules.lowAromaDesc: Missing aromatic complexity — add fresh herbs, garlic, or spices.
rules.lowSweetnessDesc: A touch of sweetness could balance the dish — try honey or caramelized onion.
rules.lowBitternessDesc: Adding a bitter element like greens or citrus zest could add complexity.
rules.highCarbsDesc: Carbohydrate content is elevated — consider replacing some starch with vegetables.
rules.lowProteinDesc: Protein is below optimal — add meat, fish, eggs, or legumes.
rules.highFatRatioDesc: Fat makes up a large share of calories — reduce oil or use leaner ingredients.
rules.lowFiberDesc: Fiber is below recommended — add vegetables, whole grains, or legumes.
rules.highSugarDesc: Sugar content is elevated — reduce sweet ingredients or use alternatives.
rules.dominanceDesc: One ingredient dominates the flavor profile — add more variety.
rules.missingFatDesc: No fat source detected — add oil, butter, or nuts for better flavor and satiety.
rules.missingAcidDesc: No acidic ingredient — add lemon, vinegar, or tomatoes to brighten the dish.
rules.missingAromaticsDesc: No aromatic herbs or spices — add garlic, herbs, or pepper.
rules.missingProteinDesc: No significant protein source — add meat, fish, eggs, or legumes.
rules.missingVegetablesDesc: No vegetables detected — add greens, roots, or other veggies for balance.
rules.high_sweetness_desc: Sweetness is very dominant — consider balancing with acid or bitter.
rules.high_acidity_desc: Acidity is very prominent — balance with fat or sweetness.
rules.high_bitterness_desc: Bitterness is strong — balance with sweetness or fat.
rules.high_umami_desc: Umami is very intense — generally fine, but can feel heavy.
rules.high_fat_desc: Fat flavor is dominant — add acid or fresh herbs to cut through.
rules.high_aroma_desc: Aroma is very strong — might overpower delicate ingredients.

success.title: Payment successful!
success.subtitle: Your actions are being credited to your account.
success.note: Stripe is confirming the payment with our server. This usually takes a few seconds — your new balance will appear in the chat shortly.

cancel.title: Checkout cancelled
cancel.subtitle: No charge was made. You can try again any time.
